Instructions.Saadiyat Lagoons HSE Scoring Template -: Use Weekly_Inspections as the response/master record after upload to Google Sheets. Users should complete only visible columns; hidden columns and dashboards update automatically.
2. Complete the visible input columns only. Hidden columns calculate automatically.
3. Score each KPI from 0 to 5. 0 = collapse of control, 5 = excellent sustained control.
4. Package and Cluster are mandatory. Contractor and responsible team auto-populate.
5. Weekly_Dashboard is for top management review. Monthly_Dashboard tracks recognition trend.
6. Gap_Analysis and CAPA_Tracker are for close-out management.

Weekly_Inspections.Weekly Inspection Entry Sheet - Google F: Complete only visible fields. Use the 0-5 dropdown for each KPI. Package and Cluster auto-fill contractor leadership and the dashboard updates automatically.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
The Construction Manager and his team were advised to ensure that sub-contractors do not mess up with their site. The following advise was given:
1. No payment for work if site condition is below the acceptable standard.
2. Close supervision of sub-contractors by the construction team of the main contractor.
3. Housekeeping shall be maintined for all shift.
4. Provide a temporary storage area for waste for each villa.
5. PTW shall remain under the control of the main contractor.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_45:
•	Work suspended for steel fixing , inside villa with yellow or red flags 
•	Permit suspended for all the villa with no adequat flagging system
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
•	•  Provide safe and proper access platforms (working platforms, access scaffolds, or mobile platforms) before starting rebar activities. 
•	•  Ensure ladders and stairs are of industrial grade, secured, and positioned at safe angles. 
•	•  Install temporary working platforms with guardrails where rebar work is done at height. 
•	•  Conduct Task Risk Assessment (TRA) specifically for rebar activities before work starts. 
•	•  Assign a competent supervisor to verify access arrangements daily. 
•	•  Include access requirements in the method statement and toolbox talks. 
•	•  Stop work immediately if safe access is not available.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_44:
•	Hot work activities were conducted without a fire watcher. 
•	Smoking was observed in an undesignated area. 
•	Materials were stored inside villas without fire sprinklers or fire extinguishers.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_45:
•	Hot work was stopped, unsafe areas were barricaded, and smoking inside the villa was prohibited immediately.
Fire extinguishers were provided, and corrective safety measures were instructed for immediate implementation.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
•	Enforce strict compliance with hot work permit procedures, including mandatory fire watcher deployment. 
•	Establish and clearly mark designated smoking areas and prohibit smoking outside these zones. 
•	Ensure all excavations, openings, and edges are permanently barricaded with proper signage. 
•	Provide complete edge protection systems, including mesh and toe boards, at all elevated areas. 
•	Restrict material storage inside villas and ensure adequate firefighting equipment is always available. 
•	Conduct regular HSE inspections, toolbox talks, and supervisory monitoring to prevent recurrence.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
•	 Fix edge protection, walkways, and cable routes. 
•	Use only trained and authorized workers. 
•	 Do regular safety inspections.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_45: High risk activity was suspended and workers removed from risk area. The PM and HSE team were invited and advised to ensure PTW implementation, close supervision and control of subcontractors.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
1.	Prepare Monthly lookahead programme for each package;
2.	Prepare Weekly lookahead programme for each cluster;
3.	Prepare Daily activity schedule for each cluster / block / villa
4.	Develop Supervision deployment plan identifying the responsible site engineers, supervisors, and HSE personnel for each work front.
5. Ensure Subcontractors' engagement in the project is strictly based on the above plans. No work shall commence without an approved PTW by the main contractor.
6. Communicate officially to all subcontractors that no activity shall commence without first reporting to the main contractor on daily basis.
7. Regularly share the above plans with KEO for close follow up and monitoring.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_44:
•	Poor Supervision
•	Inadequate Planning and Control
•	Weak Inspection, Monitoring, and Compliance
Weekly_Inspections.Column_45: Unsafe conditions were addressed immediately, work was stopped where required, and the responsible supervision team was informed to implement corrective measures and restore compliance.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
•	Strengthen site supervision by ensuring competent supervisors are present at all times to monitor activities and enforce safe work practices. 
•	Improve planning and control through comprehensive risk assessments, task planning, and strict adherence to approved procedures and MSRA. 
•	Enhance inspection and monitoring by conducting regular site inspections, audits, and timely corrective actions for any non compliance. 
•	Reinforce worker awareness via regular toolbox talks and targeted training to promote a strong safety culture and prevent recurrence.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_18: PTW was issued without the names of the authorized persons, no date or time was mentioned, no detailed description of task was included. When investigated, it was observed that the verifier from the HSE team was an inspector that was not approved for the project. The inspector does not know the requirements of PTW.

Periodic monitoring and preventive maintenance shall be implemented to ensure that plant, tools, temporary systems, and safety arrangements remain effective and do not deteriorate during the execution of the work.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_44: PTW failure / inadequate field verification.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_45:
Excavation Safety: stop unsafe excavation activity, provide safe access/egress and rigid barricading/edge protection, and verify excavation controls at site.
PTW Field Verification: suspend the activity until a valid PTW is approved, physically verified, and communicated to the work crew.
Supervision & Subcontractor Control: discuss the low KPI with the responsible site team and rectify the unsafe condition before continuing the activity.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
Excavation Safety: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall ensure excavation inspection, access/egress, barricading, permit controls, and daily closeout verification are implemented.
PTW Field Verification: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall strengthen PTW ownership by the main contractor, including field verification before approval and monitoring during execution.
Supervision & Subcontractor Control: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall assign competent supervision at the work front and verify subcontractor compliance continuously.

3. conduct periodoc inpection / focus audits

Weekly_Inspections.Column_44:
1. Welfare Arrangement is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (0/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
2. Fire Readiness is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (3/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
3. Scaffolding Compliance is one of the lowest scoring KPIs (3/5), requiring immediate site-level corrective action and management follow-up.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_45:
Welfare Arrangement: discuss the low KPI with the responsible site team and rectify the unsafe condition before continuing the activity.
Fire Readiness: reinstate fire protection resources, access, and emergency readiness immediately.
Scaffolding Compliance: prevent scaffold use until competent inspection, correction, and valid tagging are completed.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
Welfare Arrangement: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement a corrective action plan with supervision, inspection, and closeout evidence.
Fire Readiness: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall implement a corrective action plan with supervision, inspection, and closeout evidence.
Scaffolding Compliance: Project Manager and Construction Manager shall ensure scaffold planning, competent erection/inspection, tagging, and routine monitoring are enforced.

Weekly_Inspections.Column_44:
1. Look ahead program not communicated.
2. PTW not implementated
3. Poor supervision
Weekly_Inspections.Column_45: Excavation was suspended and the project team were advised on the safe approach to execute work.
Weekly_Inspections.Column_46:
Before excavation work begins, the contractor shall do the following:
1. Communicate lookahead program to the HSE team on time.
2. Ensure arrnagement for safe cross over platform and trench protection is on site before applying for excavation permit.
3. Review that arrnagement for cross over platform and trench protection is suitable and sufficient.
4. Use excavated earth to create bund walls simultaneously during excavation.
5. For excavations around villas, ensure all exit doors are blocked with hard barriers before starting excvation.
6. Do not approve excavation if the above arrangemnts are not reasonably in place.
